claim: Insights uses algorithms inspired by Anthropic's QDR paper to automatically discover patterns and failure modes in production agent traces
actionable: Use LangSmith's Insights Agent to automatically categorize agent behaviors, failure modes, and usage patterns without manually labeling traces
source_title: Why We Built LangSmith for Improving Agent Quality

claim: Threads represent user-defined units of related traces (conversations, sessions, or workflows) and are the better unit for evaluating end-to-end agent interactions
actionable: Structure your agent evals around threads (multi-turn interactions) rather than single traces when measuring user sentiment, tool trajectory, or conversation quality
source_title: Why We Built LangSmith for Improving Agent Quality

claim: Online evals measure agent performance in real-time on production data; offline evals use static datasets and serve as regression tests
actionable: Combine offline evals (regression test suite) with online evals (production monitoring) to catch regressions early and detect distribution shift
source_title: Why We Built LangSmith for Improving Agent Quality

claim: As agents reach production, tracing and observability become as important as evals; you need both discovery (Insights) and measurement (Evals)
actionable: Invest in production observability (tracing) alongside evals; use Insights to discover unknown failure modes, then formalize them as evals
source_title: Why We Built LangSmith for Improving Agent Quality

claim: Even with arbitrary agent payloads and diverse agent architectures, automatically generating insights from traces is solvable but requires good instructions and clear context
actionable: When configuring Insights Agent, provide clear descriptions of what your agent does and which patterns you want to discover; more specificity yields better results
source_title: Why We Built LangSmith for Improving Agent Quality
